channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Couple arrested after daring Empire State marriage proposal stunt
item.description: Russian daredevils Vanya Beerkus and Angela Nikolau are seen as ambassadors for 'rooftopping', a climbing sport.

item.title: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
item.description: President Trump has sought to limit mail-in voting and has ordered his administration to impose limits on the practice.

item.title: Iran football team welcomed home after World Cup exit
item.description: Iran’s football team has landed back in Tehran after their World Cup

item.title: E Jean Carroll demands Donald Trump pay $5.8m in damages from 2019 case
item.description: Carroll's lawyers have asked for an expedited resolution to the payment question after US Supreme Court declines appeal.

item.title: US signs $1 lease with Israel to build permanent embassy in West Jerusalem
item.description: The US and Israel have signed a deal allocating land for a permanent US embassy in West Jerusalem.

item.title: US Supreme Court hands wealthy donors more sway with latest decision
item.description: Critics say the ruling invites corruption, benefitting wealthy donors and amplifying special interest sway in politics.

item.title: LIVE: USA vs Bosnia and Herzegovina – FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as USA play Bosnia and Herzegovina in the round of 32 knockout stage in San Francisco.

item.title: Iran to open ‘communication channel’ on MoU with US after talks in Qatar
item.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi says part of $6bn in frozen assets would be used to buy goods Tehran needs.

item.title: Russian couple arrested after climbing Empire State Building
item.description: Two Russian climbers scaled New York’s Empire State Building on Wednesday as part of a death-defying marriage proposal.

item.title: State of emergency: Bolivia’s currency plummets as anger simmers
item.description: Bolivia’s President Paz declared a state of emergency after 50 days of protests against his policies.

item.title: Despite marquee losses, Supreme Court term grows Trump’s presidential power
item.description: Set backs on tariffs, birthright citizenship overlay broad interpretation of presidential power supported by Trump.

item.title: Israeli opposition signal foreign policy change in style, but not substance
item.description: Israeli opposition criticise the means employed by Netanyahu, but cannot risk opposing his ends.

item.title: Seven more sentenced over Texas ICE detention centre shooting
item.description: US prosecutors have alleged those involved in the Prairieland Detention Center protest were linked to antifa.

item.title: Israel kills three Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
item.description: An Israeli drone strike near a Gaza hospital and fuel station kills at least three people, reports Wafa news agency.

item.title: As Venezuela responds to earthquake devastation, volunteers take charge
item.description: As frustration with the government disaster response grows, everyday citizens are organising to distribute aid.

item.title: One week on from Venezuela’s deadly earthquakes
item.description: One week after Venezuela's twin earthquakes, more than 1,900 are confirmed dead while tens of thousands are missing.

item.title: Kane double fires England to comeback win over DR Congo at World Cup 2026
item.description: England survive huge scare against DR Congo to win 2-1 and set up last 16 clash with cohosts Mexico.

item.title: How AI is being weaponised against India’s Muslim women
item.description: For years, India's Muslim women have been subject to online abuse.

item.title: US says it won’t agree to renew USMCA
item.description: USMCA came into effect on July 1, 2020, and is slated to expire after 16 years.

item.title: Three dead after massive World Cup celebrations in Mexico City
item.description: Health officials say three people died during massive celebrations in Mexico City after Mexico’s 2-0 World Cup win.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Faith and football
item.description: From prayers on the pitch to cursing the opposing team, faith and religious belief have been prominent this World Cup.

item.title: Can Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces take control of el-Obeid?
item.description: The RSF wants to seize the strategic southern city from the Sudanese military.

item.title: Psychological toll mounts as Lebanese villages erased by Israel’s war
item.description: Displaced Lebanese face a void as war annihilates the physical and emotional anchors of their past and identity.

item.title: What military capabilities does Iran possess as it negotiates with US?
item.description: US and Israel claim extensive damage, Iran projects defiance, and satellite images show fast attempts at recovery.

item.title: The Iran talks expose the collapse of US diplomacy
item.description: In the Trump era, American diplomacy is being led not by impartial diplomats, but by real estate investors.
